---
title: "Home"
---

::: {.hero-banner}
<span class="prototype-badge">Proof of Concept</span>

<div class="hero-title">Evidence Surveillance Hub</div>

<div class="hero-subtitle">
A prototype demonstrating how living evidence surveillance outputs can be published through a searchable, downloadable, and version-controlled platform.
</div>
:::

## Purpose

The Evidence Surveillance Hub explores a simple and sustainable approach for sharing rapidly updated outbreak literature.

This prototype tests whether a GitHub repository and GitHub Pages website can provide a more accessible alternative to maintaining public evidence surveillance outputs through a spreadsheet alone.

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.info-card}
### Searchable evidence

Users can browse and filter references by outbreak, study design, and year.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Downloadable data

The underlying dataset can be downloaded as a CSV for reuse by researchers and public health professionals.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Version history

GitHub tracks changes over time, supporting transparency and reproducibility.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Low maintenance

Routine updates are designed around replacing a CSV export from DistillerSR.
:::

:::

## Proposed workflow

::: {.workflow-box}
Literature search → RefWorks → DistillerSR → CSV export → GitHub repository → GitHub Pages
:::

## Prototype scope

This proof of concept uses a sample outbreak dataset to evaluate whether this approach is technically feasible, easy to maintain, and suitable for future evidence surveillance outputs.

::: {.status-box}
**Status:** Prototype under development. This website is not intended to represent a production repository or official evidence product.
:::
